pattern: Divide the string into blocks of length k and find the most frequent block; the minimum operations is the number of non-dominant blocks.
hints: An operation replaces one k-length chunk with another; you want all chunks identical, so you keep the most frequent chunk and replace all others.
Split the word into n/k chunks of length k, count frequencies, and find the maximum frequency.
The answer is (total chunks) - (max chunk frequency) since you replace every chunk that is not the chosen dominant one.

edge_inputs.description: k equals word length - always 0 operations
k=1 means all chars must match - count non-dominant chars
tie between two most-frequent chunks - still (total - max_freq)

stress.description: random words where k divides length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    # pick k that divides length
    k = rng.randint(1, min(size, 20))
    num_chunks = rng.randint(1, size // k + 1)
    length = k * num_chunks
    word = ''.join(rng.choice('abcdefgh') for _ in range(length))
    return [word, k]
